chapter_title.en: Chapter 57 – The protection and the seeking Refuge

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/440)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/440)
text: قَالَ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) إِذَا لَقِيتَ السَّبُعَ فَقُلْ.
أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ دَانِيَالَ وَ الْجُبِّ مِنْ شَرِّ كُلِّ أَسَدٍ مُسْتَأْسِدٍ

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Jamhour, from his father, from Muhammad Bin Sinan, from Abdullah Bin Sinan,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘When you come across a predatory wild animal, so say,
‘I seek Refuge with the Lord<sup>azwj</sup> of Daniel and the Pit, from the evil of every ferocious lion’.
en.sarwar: 9. Ali ibn has narrated from ibn Jumhur from his father from Muhammad ibn Sinan from ‘Abd Allah ibn Sinan from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Amir al-Mu’minin, Ali ibn abu Talib (a.s.) has said, ‘If you come face to face with a beast say, ‘I seek protection with the Lord of Daniel and the well against the evil of all powerful lions.” Note: Daniel was thrown in a well with wild beasts but they did not harm him.
